feedback.text: I too would appreciate it if the law would include something on making sure video games (and all digital products really) stay working indefinitely when purchased. It's unacceptable from a consumer rights perspective games are purposefully broken remotely, even though people have paid real money to buy them. This practice is clearly illegal for non-digital goods, but somehow with digital goods it's not and I would love if it the EU would be able to protect its citizens better from these predatory practices.
